pattern: Encoding the set of required skills as a bitmask and each person as the skills they cover turns team-building into a shortest-path over skill-subsets, where each person is an edge that turns more bits on.
hints: There are at most a handful of skills, so the set of skills already covered fits in a single integer bitmask.
Treat each reachable skill-set as a state and each person as a transition that ORs in their skills; you want the fewest people to reach the all-skills state.
Run a DP over all 2^k skill masks: for each mask, try adding every person and keep the smallest team (list of indices) that reaches the full mask.

edge_inputs.description: second example, six skills two people
one skill, one person covers it
one person already has every skill
an empty-skill person is never useful
skills spread so two are needed

stress.description: random skill assignments over a small skill set, guaranteed solvable
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    k = rng.randint(2, max(2, min(size, 8)))
    skills = ['s' + str(i) for i in range(k)]
    # ensure coverage: scatter skills among people, then add extra noisy people
    people = []
    remaining = list(skills)
    rng.shuffle(remaining)
    while remaining:
        take = rng.randint(1, min(3, len(remaining)))
        people.append(remaining[:take])
        remaining = remaining[take:]
    extra = rng.randint(0, 6)
    for _ in range(extra):
        cnt = rng.randint(0, min(3, k))
        people.append(rng.sample(skills, cnt))
    rng.shuffle(people)
    return [skills, people]
stress.note: every skill is given to at least one person so a team always exists; k small so the 2^k DP is fast
